headline: Add your PGP key before you share your tip line
subtext: Your profile can stay visible without a key, but sources cannot send encrypted tips until your encryption setup is in place.
image_alt_text: A Hush Line social graphic built around a light-mode mobile screenshot of the Encryption settings page. The screen shows options to import a Proton Mail key or paste a public PGP key manually, alongside headline text about setting up encryption before sharing a tip line.

social.bluesky:
Before you share your Hush Line profile, add your PGP key.

Recipients can import a Proton Mail key or paste a public key manually. Your profile can still be visible without it, but people cannot send encrypted tips until encryption is set up.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
social.linkedin:
Before you share your Hush Line profile, add your PGP key.

This screen lets recipients import a Proton Mail key or paste a public key manually. Your profile can still appear in Hush Line without it, but sources cannot send encrypted tips through your form until encryption is set up.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
social.mastodon:
Before you share your Hush Line profile, add your PGP key.

Recipients can import a Proton Mail key or paste a public key manually from Settings. Your profile can still be visible without it, but people cannot send encrypted tips until encryption is set up.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

rationale: Selected the mobile encryption settings screen because recent archive history is already saturated with directory posts, including one on 2026-03-30. This candidate still addresses a documented core user need for journalists, lawyers, and other recipients preparing a safer public tip line, fits the requested mobile template, uses the latest light-mode capture set, and avoids repeating another directory theme on an adjacent posting day.
copy_brief: Write for recipients and staff using Hush Line day to day, not for platform admins.
title: Settings - Encryption (artvandelay)
